title: Caught in a Fake Breakout? Here’s a Simple Filter My Brother-in-Law Uses That Might Help
author: Massive_South_2767
selftext:
Hey everyone,
Just wanted to share something my brother-in-law showed me. He’s been testing a price action method on crude oil options that’s meant to filter out fake breakouts — those moves that trap traders before reversing.

It’s based on:
	•	Identifying volume-spike traps
	•	Avoiding entries during consolidation breakouts
	•	Waiting for confirmation after breakout + retest

He’s been seeing better results with it in Indian crude oil options, but also applied parts of it to USOIL.

I’m curious if anyone here has developed something similar to avoid fake moves — especially in volatile instruments like crude.

Would love to hear your thoughts

comments.author: FairFlowAI
comments.body:
hi 👋 
great topic 👍 yes, we trade CL futures intraday through an AI system… 

Not sure what actually the thing is that your brother in law showed you or what you actually shared in your post. 

Looks a little like AI help you to create this post… 

You are welcome to put some meat on the bone ✌️✌️

Have a good one

replies.author: Massive_South_2767
replies.body:
Hi , appreciate the enthusiasm.
Not sure what exactly you’re trying to say either — whether you’re genuinely interested or just plugging your AI system .

This isn’t AI-generated, though I get that clear writing can be mistaken for that these days. I shared what I found interesting — if you’re looking for “meat,” feel free to bring a fork too. 😉

Have a good one ✌️
